reasoning: Standard offensive rally. Joiners fill most of the troops anyway; lead keeps a balanced core so the rally doesn't collapse if joiners under-deliver.
Hard-to-counter balanced joiner composition. Infantry survives long enough for marksman to deliver damage. The most widely cited ratio across all 2025-2026 guides.
Defensive lean with some damage retained. 60/20/20 beats pure 60/40/0 for opportunistic counter-damage against unbalanced rallies.
Facility rallies use the same PvP balanced lead. Whiteout-survival.com's Foundry guide and multiple Fortress guides converge on this.
Standard joiner. In chaotic multi-rally Foundry fights you're taking constant hits so the 50% infantry buffer matters.
Defensive reinforcement. Some alliances go 60/40/0 if expecting sustained rallies with no counter-damage need.
Confirmed by the 2026 SvS formation meta. Alliance-level standard for T9/T10 attack rallies on the castle.
Default SFC joiner. Some alliances bump to 60/20/20 for sustained multi-wave fights where hospital overflow is a concern.
Consensus garrison ratio. Castle turrets target infantry first so infantry-heavy survives longer; lancers provide secondary damage. 70/30/0 is a lighter-damage alternative.
Open-world SvS flag fights use standard attack.
Standard joiner.
Heavier infantry defense during SvS when your city may be hit while offline. Per widely circulated alliance cheat sheets.
Pure PvE. Bear has fixed HP and no counter triangle so maximize lethality. Joiners bring the infantry meat shield. Server Gen 8+: push to 0/15/85 or 0/10/90 pure DPS.
gen_note: Gen 1-7: keep at least 5000 infantry in the march as a safety floor. The 0% lancer can stretch to 15-20% pre-Gen 8 if your lancer lethality is close to marksman.

reasoning: Pre-Gen 8 balance point: some infantry to absorb hits and stabilize lancer/marksman skill triggers. Gen 8+: shift to 10/15/75 as marksman lethality pulls ahead.
gen_note: Gen-dependent. Pre-Gen 8 the 25-30% lancer is common to stably proc lancer hero buffs.

reasoning: Same logic as Bear Trap — PvE boss with no counter triangle. Maximize marksman for peak damage.
Balanced joiner; enough infantry to survive across levels without being glass cannons.

Bounty missions recommend unrealistic power levels — the workaround is ratio + synergy. 20/40/40 from the 2026 Lighthouse meta beats the recommended power by 20-30M. Prior 20/20/60 advice is outdated for Expert+.

Game AI defaults to 33/33/33 which is suboptimal — override to 50/20/30 as a baseline. Labyrinth zones vary daily so expect to fine-tune. The 60/15/25 variant works well for infantry-favored zones (e.g. stage 10 which the AI itself runs at ~53/27/20).
gen_note: Experiment within 5 daily attempts; the zone's expedition buffs change the optimal ratio.

reasoning: Infantry attacks first, then lancers, then marksmen. Marksmen rarely get a turn before the wave dies so they're dead weight. Wave 10 and 20 HQ defense especially.
Same as garrison — reinforcement marches sent to allies use the same composition.
Standard AC meta; hard to counter. Works with any generation of heroes.
Standard balanced composition. The in-game default of 33/33/33 is worse — manually set sliders.
General-purpose city garrison. Heavier infantry with lancer/marksman retained to counter-damage. If specifically expecting SvS raid pressure, shift to 70/30/0.
Safest fallback when you don't know what you'll face. Multiple creators name this the 'can't go wrong' starting point.
